title: quercus alba var. pubescens
intro: Quercus alba var. pubescens, also known as the downy white oak, is a variety of the white oak tree that is native to eastern North America. The bark of this plant is rich in tannins and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is used in traditional medicine to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, inflammation, and digestive problems. Quercus alba var. pubescens can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions to take advantage of its health benefits, such as reducing the risk of heart disease and improving immune function.
